basics.name: Omkar Patil
basics.label: CS PhD @ ASU
basics.summary: I completed my Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees at IIT Madras, where I built a strong foundation in robotics and machine learning. I am currently pursuing my Ph.D. at Arizona State University (ASU) in Dr. Nakul Gopalan’s lab, focusing on robot learning, particularly methods that leverage compositionality to build generalizable manipulation skills.

publications.name: You've Got a Golden Ticket: Improving Generative Robot Policies With A Single Noise Vector
publications.publisher: Arxiv | Omkar Patil, Ondrej Biza, Thomas Weng, Karl Schmeckpeper, Wil Thomason, Xiaohan Zhang, Robin Walters, Nakul Gopalan, Sebastian Castro, Eric Rosen
publications.summary: Search method to find a single constant noise vector (golden ticket) that improves pretrained diffusion/flow matching robot policies without any retraining.

publications.name: StageCraft: Execution Aware Mitigation of Distractor and Obstruction Failures in VLA Models
publications.publisher: Arxiv | Kartikay Pangaonkar*, Prabin Rath*, Omkar Patil*, Nakul Gopalan
publications.summary: Training-free approach to improve pretrained VLA policy performance by manipulating the environment's initial state using VLM-based in-context reasoning.

publications.name: Meanings and Measurements: Multi-Agent Probabilistic Grounding for Vision-Language Navigation
publications.publisher: Arxiv | Swagat Padhan, Lakshya Jain, Bhavya Shah, Omkar Patil, Thao Nguyen, Nakul Gopalan
publications.summary: Agentic framework (MAPG) that decomposes metric-semantic language queries and probabilistically composes grounded outputs for physically consistent decisions in 3D space.

publications.name: Factorizing Diffusion Policies for Observation Modality Prioritization
publications.publisher: ICRA 2026 | Omkar Patil, Prabin Rath, Kartikay Pangaonkar, Eric Rosen, Nakul Gopalan
publications.summary: Method to prioritize observational modalities, such as vision over tactile for learning diffusion policies.

publications.name: Learning Sequential Kinematic Models from Demonstrations for Multi-Jointed Articulated Objects
publications.publisher: ICRA 2026 | Anmol Gupta, Weiwei Gu, Omkar Patil, Jun Ki Lee, Nakul Gopalan
publications.summary: Framework that learns kinematic constraints and manipulation sequences of multi-DoF objects from human demonstrations.

publications.name: Composing Diffusion Policies for Few-shot Learning of Motions
publications.publisher: Compositional Learning Workshop @ NeurIPS 2024 | Omkar Patil, Anant Sah, Nakul Gopalan
publications.summary: Compositional approach that enables few-shot learning for novel skills by utilizing a combination of base policy priors is presented.

publications.name: Hardware-Software Co-Design for Path Planning by Drones
publications.publisher: IROS 2024 | Ayushi Dube*, Omkar Patil*, Gian Singh, Nakul Gopalan, and Sarma Vrudhula
publications.summary: This work consists of designing a hardware-software co-design, MT+, for adapting the Mikami-Tabuchi (MT) algorithm for on-board path planning by drones in a 3D environment.

publications.name: Learning Temporally Composable Task Segmentations with Language
publications.publisher: IROS 2024 | Divyanshu Raj, Omkar Patil, Weiwei Gu, Chitta Baral and Nakul Gopalan
publications.summary: We present an approach to identify sub-tasks within a demonstrated robot trajectory with the supervision provided by language instructions.

publications.name: Understanding metrics for paraphrasing
publications.publisher: Arxiv | Omkar Patil, Rahul Singh, Tarun Joshi
publications.summary: We propose a novel metric ROUGE-P to measure the quality of paraphrases along the dimensions of adequacy, novelty and fluency.

publications.name: Document automation architectures and technologies: A survey
publications.publisher: Arxiv | Mohammad Achachlouei*, Omkar Patil*, Tarun Joshi, Vijayan Nair
publications.summary: This paper surveys the current state of the art in document automation in light of recent advances in AI and deep neural networks.

courses: Planning and Learning Methods for AI

work.name: Robotics and AI Institute (Formerly BDAI)
highlights: Research at the intersection of performance improvement and steering for robot policies.
Methodologies used include generative modeling (diffusion/flow) and reinforcement learning.
work.summary: Fall intern in the Compose research group, focusing on robot learning.

work.name: Arizona State University
highlights: Developing robot learning algorithms that leverage compositionality for sample-efficient generalization.
Developed a ROS2-based multi-drone formation and behavior control system in AirSim.
work.summary: Research assistant in Dr. Nakul Gopalan's lab at Arizona State University.

work.name: Wells Fargo
work.summary: Member of the Artificial Intelligence and Automation within Corporate Model Risk.
highlights: Researched and implemented prompt-tuning on language models for generating different kinds of paraphrases for downstream applications such as robustness testing.
Collaborated with other researchers within the team to develop methodologies for evaluating model weaknesses with a special focus on text classification models.

work.name: Wells Fargo
work.summary: Member of the Artificial Intelligence and Automation within Corporate Model Risk.
highlights: Explored text generation for the task of paraphrasing and developed a new metric to evaluate the quality of paraphrases.
Surveyed various document automation frameworks present in literature.
Contributed significantly to the internal code library and made several presentations on research projects, across the group

work.name: Institute WebOps and MobOps
work.summary: Lead of the official mobile development team of IIT Madras.
highlights: Led a 9 member team for the development of the ‘Students App’, managing a budget of ~INR 3L.
Increased the number of active users by ~160%, to 6500+ students, with 12000+ downloads in total.
Developed a sophisticated Java front-end and PHP back-end to build a secure and useful application on Android.

volunteer.summary: Non-governmental organization for the welfare of underprivileged children.
highlights: Quickly ramped up with the current operations of reselling scrap newspaper and facilitating education for the needy with the obtained proceeds
Streamlined the scrap collection under the ‘Pasti Ki Pathshala' project by creating a database of participants willing to donate scrap newspaper
Organized a collection drive at a local school for 800+ students to promote the idea of social welfare

volunteer.summary: Reviewer for IROS 2024, ICRA 2025, CORL 2025, ICRA 2026

skills.name: Robotics
keywords: Perception (Vision, Point Clouds), Control (Position, Operational Space)
Robotics Stack (ROS2, MuJoCo, Isaac Sim, Gazebo)

skills.name: Robot Learning
keywords: ML Tooling (PyTorch, JAX, W&B), Systems (Python, Linux, Docker, Git)
